mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 50% of rate-decline months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 65% of triggered months, or the t-statistic for the return difference versus baseline is less than 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, driving sustained positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in both series yields negative 60-day returns in more than 55% of cases, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant with p greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above 2.0 percent annualized during late-cycle expansions signals capacity constraints and impending monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: 20-day returns are positive in more than 60% of high-growth late-cycle observations, or regime classification fails to separate return distributions with a Kolmogorov-Smirnov test p-value above 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly nonfarm payroll additions exceeding 200000 confirm robust economic momentum, supporting immediate positive equity price adjustments over the following trading week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of months with over 200000 payrolls, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month cumulative CPI increase surpassing 1.5 percent triggers inflation fears and expectations of aggressive rate hikes, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered periods, or the inflation threshold fails to predict return direction with a logistic regression coefficient p-value above 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly retail sales, where retail sales outpace GDP by over 1.0 percent, indicates strong consumer-driven demand that lifts equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: The retail-GDP spread yields negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of observations, or the interaction effect is indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Negative industrial production growth during a monetary tightening cycle confirms economic deceleration, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day returns are positive in more than 65% of tightening-cycle contraction months, or the regime filter adds no predictive power with a nested model F-test p-value above 0.10.
